title: Perioperative Patient Flow
des_qualification_note: All required criteria satisfied: discrete patients and surgical teams flow through pre-op/intra-op/post-op phases; discrete events (admission, OR entry, incision, closure, PACU transfer, discharge); explicit simulated clock across OR days and shifts. Both typical criteria present: ORs, PACU beds, surgical teams, nursing staff are finite resources with contention; procedure durations, patient arrivals, and emergency interruptions are stochastic.
summary_of_content_provenance: Rich-but-friction-light healthcare DES source landscape. Three first-party fetches: one clean PMC fetch (PMC7333415, orthopedic OR setup ergonomics using Delmia), one Wikipedia definitional reference (three-phase perioperative framework), and one INFORMS WSC 2012 paper with extraction_status=extracted_partial (PDF returned high-level structure but not numeric detail). Multiple paywalled candidates (Hassanzadeh 2023 Wiley, Pu 2024, Springer workflow study) flagged for human follow-up. This is the first walkthrough run under the schema-aware simvault-seeder skill; tagging was schema-native from day one. All 12 modeling feature tags came from the canonical seed with zero new feature proposals — validates the 27-feature seed covers perioperative flow without gaps.
intended_audience_note: Healthcare operations managers, OR directors, and perioperative nurse managers. Scenarios reflect real decisions about OR utilization, staffing, and patient flow bottlenecks.
